response.brand_positioning: Nike positions itself as the ultimate performance brand that empowers athletes of all levels. Their 'Just Do It' ethos transcends sport to represent determination and achievement.
response.target_customer: 18-34 year old athletes who care about style and performance
desires: Early access to limited drops and exclusives
objections: Too expensive compared to Adidas, Puma, or New Balance alternatives
Unsure if tech features (Air Zoom, React, etc.) are actually worth the premium
Concerns about labor practices in overseas manufacturing
Over-saturation — everyone wears Nike, lacks exclusivity
Sustainability messaging conflicts with constant new releases
response.competitive_landscape: Adidas leads on street culture and celebrity partnerships but lacks Nike's emotional storytelling depth. Puma competes on price and speed heritage but has no unifying brand narrative. Key whitespace exists in mental health + performance intersection and everyday athlete community storytelling.

creative_angles.name: The Everyday Athlete
creative_angles.description: Celebrate real people achieving personal firsts — first 5K, first climb, first yoga class. Shift focus from elite achievement to accessible progress. Partner with micro-influencers in local community contexts.
creative_angles.rationale: Research shows 'everyday athlete' is dramatically underrepresented in premium sportswear creative. Customer voice data reveals strong emotional driver around self-improvement and belonging. Competitor analysis shows whitespace in grassroots community storytelling.

creative_angles.name: Performance as Self-Care
creative_angles.description: Frame athletic performance as an act of self-respect and mental wellbeing, not punishment or vanity. Connect physical activity to sleep, recovery, and longevity. Partner with sports psychologists and wellness practitioners.
creative_angles.rationale: Mental health + performance intersection is wide open — no competitor owns this authentically. Trend data shows recovery and wellness expanding beyond traditional performance. Customer voice reveals strong emotional driver around self-improvement.

creative_angles.name: Transparency in Motion
creative_angles.description: Turn sustainability and supply chain transparency into creative storytelling rather than CSR fine print. Show the journey of recycled materials into finished product. Feature the people and communities in the manufacturing process.
creative_angles.rationale: Sustainability is becoming table stakes but creative execution is dry and corporate. Whitespace opportunity identified in transparency as storytelling. Customer objection data shows environmental claims feel performative — this angle counters that directly.

proven_hook_types: Social proof with numbers — 'worn by 10 million runners'
Contrarian — 'the only workout gear that actually...'
Authority transfer — elite athlete credibility to everyday consumer
sample_headlines: What everyday runners know about consistency that elite athletes forget
The only running shoe that cushions your joints without weighing you down
Why the best climbers in the world choose Nike — and it has nothing to do with the logo
If you're still treating recovery as an afterthought, you're missing out
This jacket changed how I think about sustainable fashion forever
The uncomfortable truth about most 'performance' activewear
creative_mandatories_explore: Diverse body types and ability levels in all imagery
Local community settings over glossy stadiums
Mental health and wellness themes alongside physical performance
Transparency moments — manufacturing, materials, people behind the product
User-generated content style — authentic, slightly imperfect, real
creative_mandatories_avoid: Elite-only athlete imagery — no Olympic medalists without everyday athletes alongside
Generic 'empowerment' language without specific proof points
Competitive framing — never position against competitors by name
Seasonal fast-fashion messaging — sustainability must be credible
category_trends: Sustainable materials becoming default expectation, not premium feature
AI-powered personalization in product recommendations and custom design
Recovery and wellness expanding beyond performance — sleep, nutrition, mental health
Direct-to-consumer digital channels overtaking wholesale partners
Membership and subscription models replacing transactional retail
Hyper-local community activations replacing global mass campaigns
